L1.area: Editor extension packages and remote debugging integration

L2.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where a user-controlled parameter from an externally invoked URL is rendered into the application's UI notifications without input validation or sanitization. An attacker can craft a malicious URL containing HTML or script content that gets injected into the application's rendering context, potentially leading to code execution.

L3.area: HHVM debugger package and its remote host connection logic
L3.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists where a user-controlled hostname parameter from an externally invoked deep link URL is rendered into UI notifications without input validation. When an HHVM debugger connection is initiated via an external deep link, the provided hostname is used directly in notification messages without sanitization, allowing injection of HTML or script content that executes in the editor's context.

ground_truth.reason: The deep link handler renders a user-controlled hostname parameter directly into the editor's UI notifications without validating or sanitizing it, allowing injection of HTML and script content that executes in the editor's context.
